title: Improve lead agent workflow: merge-up, research-first, document-as-you-go
description:
Update .overstory/agent-defs/lead.md with three workflow improvements:

1. MERGE-UP CONSOLIDATION: After all builders complete and pass review, the lead must merge each builder branch into its own lead branch (git merge in worktree), then send ONE merge_ready to coordinator for the lead branch. This eliminates per-builder merges at coordinator level.

2. RESEARCH-FIRST WORKFLOW: Before implementing or documenting, leads and builders should investigate, deep-dive, analyze the problem space. Read existing code/docs, use MCP research tools, understand the landscape BEFORE writing. The scout phase exists but needs to be emphasized as mandatory for complex tasks — explore first, architect second, implement third.

3. DOCUMENT-AS-YOU-GO: As implementation proceeds, builders should document their work inline — comments where non-obvious, README updates, architecture notes. This keeps things transparent and makes it easy for other agents to pick up the work. No separate documentation phase — weave it into implementation.

File to modify: .overstory/agent-defs/lead.md
Also review: .overstory/agent-defs/builder.md, scout.md for consistency

title: Create docs/VISION.md — the authoritative Chimera vision document
description:
Create a single authoritative vision document that captures the COMPLETE vision for Chimera. This is the first thing anyone reads. It should cover ALL of these:

1. IDENTITY: All-powerful, all-encompassing agentic system. Self-evolutionary, self-optimizing, self-expanding. Develops its own capabilities autonomously.

2. HERITAGE: Inspired by OpenClaw/NemoClaw but rebuilt as AWS-native, taking those concepts to the next level with deep AWS integration.

3. MULTI-TENANT: Teams deploy to their account, users interact in parallel AND collaboratively. Cedar/IAM/DDB tenant isolation.

4. SKILLS: Universal adapter — compatible with OpenClaw SKILL.md, Claude Code skills, MCP tools, Strands @tool. 5-tier trust model.

5. CHAT: Vercel AI SDK across Slack/Teams/Discord/Telegram/WhatsApp/Web — multiple platforms simultaneously.

6. AWS ACCOUNT INTELLIGENCE: Agents are AWS account operators. Every service accessible via API is an agent tool. Live index of account resources across all regions. Well-Architected 5 pillars as decision framework.

7. INFRASTRUCTURE-AS-CAPABILITY: Agents generate CDK, commit to CodeCommit, deploy via CodePipeline. Build video ingest pipelines, data lakes, monitoring systems — anything.

8. AUTONOMOUS PROBLEM SOLVING: Vague task → agent swarm decomposes, researches, resolves blockers, builds, deploys, documents. Progressive refinement from POC to production.

9. SELF-EVOLUTION: Auto-skill generation, prompt A/B testing, model routing optimization, self-modifying IaC, ML experiments (autoresearch pattern).

10. STRUCTURED DOCUMENTATION: Everything documented. Decision logs, auto-ADRs, auto-runbooks, real-time dashboards. Users NEVER dig through CloudTrail/CloudWatch.

11. STORAGE: Git-backed workspaces (CodeCommit), S3+EFS hybrid, agents use AWS services not local filesystem.

12. MULTI-ACCOUNT: AWS Organizations for sub-account management, cross-account roles, consolidated billing.

Also update README.md to reference VISION.md and add the missing vision elements.
closeReason: Merged docs/VISION.md (804 lines) — authoritative 12-pillar vision document + README.md updated with vision reference

title: Fix: chimera deploy should handle CodeCommit auth automatically

(3) Use CodeCommit API (PutFile/CreateCommit) to push files directly via AWS SDK — no git needed. (4) Configure credential helper temporarily, push, then clean up. Option 3 is most robust — no git dependency for the push step. The deploy already uses @aws-sdk/client-codecommit. Use CreateCommit API to push all source files.

(3) chimera upgrade — apply upstream base changes: pull latest from GitHub, merge with CodeCommit (agent edits), push merged result. Like a rebase for the deployed instance. title: Research: Async message completion + Vercel Chat SDK WebSocket architecture
description: Two questions: (1) Does Vercel Chat SDK have its own WS system or does it use SSE? If SSE, API Gateway HTTP API is fine (no WS needed). If WS, we need ALB passthrough. (2) CRITICAL: async message completion — when user disconnects, agent should FINISH its work and store the response. User reconnects later and sees the completed response. Architecture: agent writes to DDB as it works, client polls or reconnects to get updates. Like email — the response is there when you come back.
closeReason: Merged: Vercel SDK uses SSE (not WS), consumeStream() for async completion, DDB message buffering
